description: Accessing a post-transition variant field after discriminating the source variant but WITHOUT calling transition() fires E-TYPE-LIFECYCLE-VARIANT-NOT-TRANSITIONED (§14.12.6.2). The cell `phase` has the variant-progression lifecycle `(.Draft to .Published)`; inside `if (@phase is .Draft)` the source variant is discriminated, but `@phase.publishedAt` reads the post-transition `.Published` field before `transition(phase)` is called, so the type system reports the error. Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §14.12.6.2 under a variant-progression lifecycle, discriminating the source variant is not enough to read a post-transition field; `transition(binding)` must be called first, else E-TYPE-LIFECYCLE-VARIANT-NOT-TRANSITIONED fires. The top-level (V5-strict) `<phase>: (.Draft to .Published)` declaration form is load-bearing for the reproducer.
